states.name: Antares
states.title: Thunderhearted Heroine
states.description:
Antares has always been your average girl. 

Her grades are good enough not to disappoint, but she's far from the top of the class. She's pretty in a cute kind of way, but she doesn't turn heads. She's well-liked among her classmates, but not truly popular. 

She blends in so well that at times, she's afraid she'll disappear. 

But jealousy doesn't come naturally to her, and she can never stay mopey for longer than a day or two. Whenever she starts to feel down, she just looks around for what she loves. 

Even in a dying world, she finds things that make her smile. And even if she doesn't realize it, that is a remarkable gift.

states.name: Antares
states.title: Thunderhearted Heroine
states.description:
The thing about discovering your destiny is that you're never truly ready. Everyone's life is average until the moment it isn't -- and none have learned this better than Antares. 

She doesn't know if the Goddess influences fate itself, or if she's just very talented at stringing together coincidences, but as it would happen, her path crossed Mirai Narukami's totally by accident. By meeting her, she discovered the sinister plot of a foreign god looking to brainwash the world. 

And by discovering that plot, she found the power that slept inside her all along. The Goddess's Hope. 

As timid and unsure as she is, only she can save the world.

states.name: Antares
states.title: Thunderhearted Heroine
states.description:
There have been dozens of recorded Requiems, stretching back to the beginning of human history. Though they started off as earth-shattering wars between demigods, the establishment of the Goddess's Church helped bring the Vessels under a greater authority.  

For the last thousand years, the names and Gifts of each girl have been recorded, to ensure they are honored after their deaths. The specific Gifts the Goddess passes down vary with each cycle, but most make frequent returns, sometimes performing important deeds before passing on.  

Interestingly, Antares's gift has never once appeared. Perhaps that is why the Church has yet to track her down. They have never once looked in a Vessel's eyes and seen a glimmer of hope.
